---
title: "Our Project"
format: html
self-contained: true

about:
  template: jolla
  image: ./pictures/_methode_times_prod_web_bin_31bac9d5-25be-4cef-9745-b75c82eb24d0.png
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/orgs/lse-ds105/teams/my-team
---

<style>
body {
  background-color: #f0f7ff;
}
</style>



## Our Team

<strong>Deniz Ozayaz |</strong> Bsc Politics and Data Science

<strong>Blanche Boomla |</strong> Bsc Phsychological and Behavioural Sciences

<strong>Amelie Byrne |</strong> Bsc Phsychological and Behavioural Sciences

<strong>Ibrahim Ghazali |</strong>Bsc Economics



## Research Question
<figure class="text-center" style="position: relative; margin: auto; 50px; top: 0px; max-width: 600px;margin-bottom: 75px;">
  <blockquote class="blockquote" style="color: #FEFEFE; font-size: 40px; background-color: rgba(3, 82, 128, 0.5); padding: 20px; border-radius: 50px;">
    <p class="mb-0">Can we use social media data to provide inisghts into voters political preferences in the US? </p>
  </blockquote>
</figure>

<body>
    <img src="Unknown.jpeg" alt="">
</body>

## Our Mission

<figure class="text-center" sstyle="display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; max-width: 600px; margin-bottom: 75px;">
  <blockquote class="blockquote" style="color: #FEFEFE; font-size: 25px; background-color: rgba(3, 82, 128, 0.5); padding: 20px; border-radius: 50px;">
    <p class="mb-0">Our mission is to unlock the potential of social media data to highlight voter trends within the US political system.
    
  We hope to use webscraping using APIs and data visualisation techniques to present the patterns of the subreddit data.

  We envision that our insights would provide presidential candidates with state unique data on political and social preferences. Data that would hopefully be invaluable during the course of a presedential campaign.
   </p>
  </blockquote>
</figure>
